bibliographic_data.title: LiDAR-Based Place Recognition For Autonomous Driving: A Survey
bibliographic_data.abstract: This paper provides a comprehensive review of LiDAR-based Place Recognition (LPR) methods, which enable vehicles to identify previously visited locations despite variations in appearance, weather, and viewpoints. The authors explore the relationship between PR and autonomous driving components, delve into the problem formulation of LPR, challenges, and relations to previous surveys, offer detailed classifications, strengths and weaknesses, and architectures of related research, summarize existing datasets and evaluation metrics, and envision promising future directions.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper provides a comprehensive review of LiDAR-based Place Recognition (LPR) methods for autonomous driving, addressing the gap in systematic reviews on LPR.
core_content.key_methodology: The authors conduct an in-depth review of related research, offering detailed classifications, strengths and weaknesses, and architectures. They also summarize existing datasets and evaluation metrics.
outcomes: Strengths and weaknesses analysis of LPR methods
core_content.full_text_markdown: LiDAR has gained popularity in autonomous driving due to its advantages like long measurement distance, rich 3D information, and stability in harsh environments. Place Recognition (PR) enables vehicles to identify previously visited locations despite variations in appearance, weather, and viewpoints, even determining their global location within prior maps. This capability is crucial for accurate localization in autonomous driving. Consequently, LiDAR-based Place Recognition (LPR) has emerged as a research hotspot in robotics. However, existing reviews predominantly concentrate on Visual Place Recognition (VPR), leaving a gap in systematic reviews on LPR. This paper bridges this gap by providing a comprehensive review of LPR methods, thus facilitating and encouraging further research.
